question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of caching on the `health()` method to cause every Spring Actuator poll (which may occur every few seconds) to trigger a full reactive pipeline evaluation including an external HTTP call to `/network/stake`, amplifying load on the REST API backend and causing a non-network-based resource exhaustion?
